title:
Design System
in Claude Code
subtitle: An AI-first design system — shared by designers, developers, and AI.

slides.title:
Design System
in Claude Code
slides.description: Tokens, components, and rules shared by designers, developers, and AI — one source of truth governing scanner, web, and every surface in between.

slides.label: 01 · The Shift · Context
slides.title: From design systems to infrastructure
slides.content: Design systems used to be libraries. Now they're infrastructure — shared governance that designers, developers, and AI operate inside.
issues: Components → systems that collaborate with designers
Figma library → code as the source of truth
slides.highlight: The system stopped being a reference. It became the infrastructure.

slides.label: 02 · System Architecture · Foundation
slides.title: Shared by designers, developers, and AI
slides.content: Storybook, design tokens, and product surfaces all governed by the same constraints — with Claude Code as the connective layer.
slides.highlight: All governed by the same constraints.

image.caption: System architecture — tokens, Storybook, surfaces, AI rules

slides.label: 03 · The System · Tokens, Components, Variants
slides.title: One connected library
slides.content: A full design system built in code — tokens at the foundation, components on top, and variants that flex to every context. Every piece connected, governed, and shared across designers, developers, and AI.
issues: Design tokens — colour, typography, spacing, motion
Core components — buttons, inputs, modals, cards, tables
Variants for every state, size, platform, and theme
Living docs generated alongside every component
slides.highlight: Consistency was a baseline. Extensibility was the goal.

slides.label: 04 · Multi-Platform · Scanner and Web
slides.title: Same component. Two contexts.
slides.content: Scanner is used with gloves, at arm's length — it needs bigger targets and touch-first interactions. Web is compact and mouse-driven. One library, a platform switch scales every component.
issues: Scanner mode — bigger targets, touch-first, gloved hands
Web mode — compact, dense, keyboard and mouse
Switching is a platform flag — not a rebuild
slides.highlight: One system, two shapes — same logic underneath.

slides.label: 05 · Theming · Dark and Light Mode
slides.title: One component. Every theme.
slides.content: Every component reads from the token layer — dark and light aren't separate builds, just the same component responding to a theme change.
slides.highlight: Theming stopped being a project. It became a property of the system.

image.caption: Same components — dark and light themes

slides.label: 06 · AI as a Collaborator · Ways of Working
slides.title: AI didn't replace the work. It amplified it.
slides.content: Claude Code operated as a structured design collaborator — working through explicit rules, prompts, and proven methodologies. Designers owned intent. AI accelerated execution.
slides.highlight: AI did not replace human work. It amplified capabilities and reduced friction.

image.caption: Designer and AI — a structured collaboration

slides.label: 07 · Outcomes · What Changed
slides.title: What this project reflects about design today.

outcomes.title: Iteration
outcomes.description: Prototyping in the real stack — sprints shrink to hours.

outcomes.title: Breaking changes
outcomes.description: Shared governance catches drift before it ships.

outcomes.title: Designer onboarding
outcomes.description: New designers inherit the rules — the system teaches itself.

outcomes.title: The designer's role
outcomes.description: Less drawing, more defining — the system is the deliverable.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.subtitle: This project reflects how my role as a designer has changed.
